data.title: Goldman Sachs Analysts Note Rebound in Investor Risk Appetite
data.description: Investor risk appetite has returned to levels seen before the Iran conflict, according to Goldman Sachs analysts. ChainCatcher reports that the stock market's volatility has significantly decreased as investors bet against the worst-case scenario in the U.S.-Iran tensions. Retail investors in the U.S. are re-entering the stock market, and automated trading is contributing to the market's rise. In the four weeks leading up to last Wednesday, investors withdrew nearly $125 billion from money market funds, marking one of the largest outflows on record.

data.title: Strive CEO Responds to Grayscale Report on Bitcoin's Impact on Dividends
data.description: Strive CEO ColeMacro has responded to a Grayscale report, highlighting its assessment of Strategy's ability to pay long-term STRC dividends based on Bitcoin price increases. According to ChainCatcher, ColeMacro emphasized the significant demand for digital credit, noting its double-digit returns, low volatility, and appeal to investors who prefer credit risk investments. He also criticized the report for not addressing volatility and lacking in credit analysis.

data.title: Strive CEO responds to Grayscale report: STRC dividends depend on Bitcoin price increases.
data.description: Strive CEO ColeMacro responded to the Grayscale report, stating that it indicated Strategy's long-term ability to pay STRC dividends depends on rising Bitcoin prices. Macro stated that there is huge demand for digital credit because it offers double-digit returns, low volatility, and is easily understood and favored by credit risk investors. He also pointed out that the report failed to discuss volatility and had gaps in its credit analysis. (BitcoinTreasuries.NET)

data.title: ING Analysts Warn of Slowing Potential Growth in Eurozone
data.description: ING analysts Ruben Dewitt and Peter Vanden Houte have indicated that potential growth in the Eurozone is likely to slow due to suppressed productivity. According to Jin10, the analysts stated in a report that unless there is a sudden acceleration in productivity, the potential GDP growth rate could fall below 1%. They highlighted that while productivity has been a significant driver of growth in the United States, it remains weak in Europe. Italy and Germany even experienced negative productivity growth during 2024-25. Expanding the labor force could be a key driver, as evidenced by increased immigration in Spain. However, they noted that due to political resistance, this is unlikely to serve as a model for other parts of Europe. Revitalizing productivity will require ambitious structural policies, but progress is expected to be very slow.

data.title: Aave will vote on whether to suspend token buybacks in order to prioritize resolving the rsETH issue.
data.description: The Snapshot page shows that TokenLogic, an Aave ecosystem service provider, has initiated a governance proposal regarding "whether to suspend AAVE buybacks." The proposal suggests suspending the AAVE buyback program until the situation surrounding the rsETH incident becomes clearer, with the resumption date to be assessed separately. Voting will begin tomorrow (April 28th) and end on May 1st.

data.title: Gate Router launches GateRouter enterprise account feature, creating a closed loop for AI usage and governance.
data.description: Gate announced the launch of GateRouter Enterprise Account functionality, enhancing enterprise-level management capabilities on top of its existing AI model gateway and providing an integrated AI usage and governance solution for teams and organizations. GateRouter supports rapid access to over 30 mainstream models, including GPT, Claude, DeepSeek, and Gemini, via a single API, and automatically matches models based on task complexity. Through intelligent routing that automatically selects cost-effective models, GateRouter helps enterprises significantly reduce costs and achieve more efficient scalable applications. This launch of GateRouter Enterprise Account functionality optimizes three aspects: cost, permissions, and data. It achieves unified billing through a shared quota pool and controls budgets using a three-tiered limit mechanism of organization, member, and API Key; it supports up to four levels of organizational structure and a multi-role permission system for refined management; and it provides multi-dimensional statistics such as average usage per user, model distribution, and API Key, making AI usage traceable and analyzable. The overall design integrates model invocation with organizational management, providing fundamental support for the large-scale deployment of AI within enterprises.

data.title: Germany's Chancellor Merz: Iran's Strength Exceeds Expectations in War
data.description: Germany's Chancellor Merz commented on the ongoing conflict involving Iran, stating that the capabilities of the Iranian forces have clearly surpassed initial expectations. According to Jin10, Merz's remarks highlight the underestimated strength of Iran in the current war scenario. The statement comes amid heightened tensions and ongoing military engagements in the region.

data.title: ECB Staff Criticizes EU's Capital Requirements Compared to US Standards
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) staff has expressed concerns over the European Union's approach to capital requirements for large banks, stating that the US imposes stricter standards. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ECB's pushback against industry arguments advocating for weaker regulations in the EU. The ECB staff's comments come amid ongoing discussions about financial regulations and their impact on the banking sector. The comparison between the US and EU standards is part of a broader debate on how to ensure financial stability while fostering economic growth. The ECB's stance underscores the importance of maintaining robust regulatory frameworks to prevent financial crises and protect consumers.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Reuters Survey: Gold Prices Expected to Average $4,916 in 2026
data.description: Gold prices are projected to average $4,916 per ounce in 2026, according to a Reuters survey. The survey also forecasts an average price of $5,100 per ounce for 2027. According to Jin10, these projections reflect expectations of continued demand and market dynamics influencing the precious metals sector.

data.title: Commerzbank: The yen may fall if the Bank of Japan does not clearly signal an interest rate hike.
data.description: Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Bauer said in a report that the yen could weaken if the Bank of Japan keeps interest rates unchanged on Tuesday and remains cautious about future rate hikes. He stated that the market expects the Bank of Japan to give a fairly clear signal that a rate hike is possible in June—at least assuming a de-escalation of the conflict with Iran by then. If this signal fails to materialize, the Ministry of Finance's warnings about intervention to support the yen will "no longer be very helpful." Bauer said that in this scenario, the USD/JPY exchange rate could rise above 160. (Jinshi)

data.title: Analyst Warns of Waning Optimism Amid U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description: Analyst Jonas Goltermann from Capital Economics has expressed concerns over the ongoing U.S.-Iran tensions and the potential prolonged closure of the Strait of Hormuz. According to Odaily, Goltermann noted that optimism regarding a resolution to the conflict may soon diminish if the situation persists. He stated, "Negotiations to end the U.S.-Iran conflict and reopen the Strait of Hormuz appear to be stalled again, prompting global financial markets to adopt a cautious stance at the start of the new week. Time is not on the side of optimists: with each passing day, disruptions to the energy market intensify, causing greater downstream damage to global economies."

data.title: Adobe Shares Decline as Mizuho Downgrades Rating
data.description: Adobe's stock fell by 1.2% in pre-market trading. According to Jin10, Mizuho Bank downgraded its rating on Adobe from 'outperform' to 'neutral.'

data.title: Tech Giants Plan $650 Billion Capital Spending Amid Helium Supply Concerns
data.description: Alphabet, Amazon, Meta, and Microsoft are projecting approximately $650 billion in capital expenditures by 2026. According to NS3.AI, this ambitious forecast comes as the helium supply, crucial for semiconductor manufacturing, faces tightening constraints. The situation has been exacerbated by Iran's recent actions affecting Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, a key helium production site. Additionally, Russia has implemented helium export controls, which will remain in place through the end of 2027, further intensifying supply challenges.

data.title: Japanese Yen May Weaken if Bank of Japan Maintains Rates
data.description: German Commerzbank analyst Volkmar Bauer stated in a report that the Japanese yen might weaken if the Bank of Japan keeps interest rates unchanged on Tuesday and remains cautious about future rate hikes. According to Odaily, Bauer noted that the market expects the Bank of Japan to provide a clear signal of a potential rate hike in June, assuming the Iran conflict eases by then. If such a signal does not materialize, warnings from Japan's Ministry of Finance about intervening to support the yen will "no longer be of much help." Bauer indicated that in this scenario, the USD/JPY exchange rate could rise above 160.

data.title: Cryptocurrency stocks generally rose in pre-market trading in the US, with MSTR up 1.02%.
data.description: According to data from msx.com, crypto stocks generally rose in pre-market trading in the US, with COIN up 0.42%, MSTR up 1.02%, ABTC up 0.81%, HOOD up 0.08%, and MARA up 0.17%. msx.com is a decentralized RWA trading platform that has listed hundreds of RWA tokens, covering US stock and ETF tokens such as AAPL, AMZN, GOOGL, META, MSFT, NFLX, and NVDA.

data.title: Capital Economics: US-Iran negotiations stalled, market optimism may soon fade.
data.description: Capital Economics analyst Jonas Goltman stated that if the US-Iran stalemate continues and the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed, market optimism about an imminent resolution to the Iran war could soon begin to wane. The analyst said, "Negotiations to end the US-Iran war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz appear to have stalled again, causing global financial markets to adopt a cautious stance at the start of the new week. Time is not on the side of optimists: with each passing day, the disruption to energy markets intensifies, causing greater downstream damage to economies worldwide." (Jinshi)

data.title: With 45 days to go until the 2026 World Cup, Polymarket reports that France's chances of winning have surpassed Spain's.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer, in Polymarket's "2026 FIFA World Cup Winner" prediction event, France's probability of winning has overtaken Spain's, currently ranking first with 17%. Spain is second with 15%, England has 11%, and Argentina and Brazil are both at 9%. To date, the total trading volume for this prediction event has exceeded $770 million. The contract rules state that the final result of the national team winning the 2026 FIFA World Cup will be immediately settled as "No" if, according to FIFA rules, the team has no chance of winning the World Cup (e.g., being eliminated in the knockout stage). The settlement is primarily based on official FIFA information, but also considers consensus from other reliable reports. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Banking Circle Launches Stablecoin Settlement Service
data.description: Banking Circle, a Luxembourg-based bank, has introduced a stablecoin settlement service offering two-way settlement between fiat currencies and stablecoins. According to Foresight News, the bank received a Crypto Asset Service Provider (CASP) license from the Luxembourg Financial Supervisory Authority (CSSF) on April 15. The service currently supports instant settlement for USDC, USDG, and EURI, aiming to enhance the efficiency of cross-border settlements and fund management through blockchain technology.

data.title: Hong Kong SFC Clarifies Prediction Markets Are Not Investment Products
data.description: Hong Kong's Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) investor education committee has clarified that transactions and contracts within prediction markets are not classified as investment products. According to NS3.AI, the committee emphasized that individuals participating in these markets are not afforded protection under the Securities and Futures Ordinance or the regulations enforced by the SFC. The committee also cautioned that seeking recourse could be challenging or even impossible if issues occur.

data.title: Western Union will launch its "Stable Card," a Solana-based stablecoin card, next month.
data.description: Western Union announced during its first-quarter earnings call that it will launch its Solana-based stablecoin, USDPT, next month. First announced in October 2025, the stablecoin is now in its "final stages." Anchorage Digital Bank will issue USDPT, a stablecoin designed specifically for Western Union's global agent network for settlement purposes, not for consumers. Western Union plans to launch USD stablecoin cards in "dozens of markets" later this year, allowing consumers to hold value in stablecoin form and spend globally. (Decrypt)
